Cambridge City
MA

Resolution
RES 2026 #1

Resolution on the Death of Anne Smaglia.

Information

Department:City Clerk's Office, PNSponsors:Councillor Patricia Nolan
Category:Death Resolution

Attachments

Version History
  1. Printout

Body

WHEREAS:

The City Council was saddened to learn of the death of Anne Elizabeth Smaglia (Quigley), on December 11 at age 76; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Anne was born on April 21, 1949, and spent her childhood moving often, living in Massachusetts, Virginia, Mississippi, France, Georgia, and New York, before graduating from the Massachusetts General Hospital School of Nursing and living for several years on Western Avenue in Cambridge; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Anne’s professional life was devoted to nursing, working in several roles around the country, serving patients with warmth, compassion, and clinical excellence; and to her colleagues Anne was known as an excellent critical care nurse and a trusted presence, able to handle the most challenging cases with compassion and competence; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Anne was a devoted wife, mother, and grandmother, whose love and kindness were always present in her gatherings of friends and family that were filled with wonderful food, laughter, and connection; and whose passion for cooking, reading, and finding the perfect piece of sea glass will be remembered by all her loved ones; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Anne was the mother of Christine Smaglia and mother-in-law of Melissa Peters, the Assistant City Manager for Community Development, both residents of Cambridge, and the loving and beloved grandmother of Remy; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Anne will missed dearly by her family, including her husband Robert and her siblings Kathleen, Elizabeth, and Edward who were blessed with many years of love and kindness; and predeceased by her sisters Mary and Margaret Quigley (of Cambridge), and her warmth, generosity, and strength will live on through the countless lives she touched; now therefore be it

 

 

RESOLVED:

That the City Council go on record expressing its deepest condolences to the family of Anne Smaglia, for their tremendous loss; and be it further

 

 

RESOLVED:

That the City Clerk be and hereby is requested to forward a suitably engrossed copy of this resolution to the family of Anne Smaglia on behalf of the entire City Council.
